source: NvChad - NvChad UI: Built-In Plugins: the base plugins NvChad ships and the init.lua vs ui.lua split

questions.q: Which plugin naming convention does this chapter use?
questions.why: The user_github/plugin_name form avoids ambiguity between similar names.

questions.q: Within NvChad's plugins folder, what do init.lua and ui.lua handle?
options: init.lua = functionality plugins; ui.lua = editor appearance
questions.why: init.lua configures functionality; ui.lua sets colors/icons/appearance.

options: A library of common Lua functions used by other plugins (telescope, gitsigns)
questions.why: plenary is a shared Lua utility library.

options: git decorations (added/removed/changed lines), also shown in the statusline
a full git GUI like lazygit
questions.why: gitsigns shows git change signs in the gutter and statusline.

options: manage installation of language servers/tools via a graphical interface
questions.why: Mason simplifies installing LSP servers and tools.

options: completion (autocomplete), fed by sources like LuaSnip, cmp-nvim-lsp, cmp-buffer, cmp-path
questions.why: nvim-cmp is the completion engine with several source plugins.

options: Automatically inserts the closing bracket/quote when you type an opening one
questions.why: Typing ( auto-inserts ) with the cursor in the middle.

options: advanced fuzzy file search (and e.g. :Telescope themes)
questions.why: Telescope is the fuzzy finder; it can even pick NvChad themes.
